name: Tex-Mex Black Bean Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix beans in a bowl and partially mash until chunky. Set aside.
2. Heat oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium heat.
3. Add onions and garlic; saute for 4 minutes or until tender.
4. Add beans, tomatoes, picante sauce, cumin, and chili powder to the skillet.
5. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until thickened.
6. Remove from heat and stir in cheese and cilantro. Let cool slightly before serving.
7. Serve warm or at room temperature with fat-free corn or flour tortilla chips or cut-up veggies.
Makes about 2-1/2 cups. The recipe can be multiplied. When made with reduced-fat cheese, each tablespoon contains approximately 1 calorie, 1.3 grams of fat.
